summary: Core wrapper around gotd/td's Telegram client: manages session directory setup, session storage, auth-check-on-connect, and exposes the live tg.Client API handle

requires: apiID and apiHash credentials supplied at construction
guarantees: Session directory created (0700) before any network activity
Function f is only called after successful authentication
Returns a composite error covering connection, auth, and f errors

effects: Delegates session file I/O to FileSessionStorage
state: c.client and c.api are nil until Run() is called; API() called before Run() returns nil with no indication of error

observations.text: API() returns nil if called before or after Run() — callers that store the *tg.Client reference and use it outside the Run callback will panic on method calls

Client construction (telegram.NewClient) is deferred to Run(), not NewClient() — the struct is inert until Run() is invoked. This means the same Client instance cannot be reused across multiple Run() calls safely (c.client is overwritten each time).

HINT MATCH — 8 inbound calls from main.go confirm this is the primary integration point for all Telegram operations during initialization. The Run/callback pattern means main orchestrates timing; client owns auth and connection.
Auth check uses c.client.Auth().Status() before invoking authenticate() — already-authenticated sessions skip the interactive flow entirely, enabling headless operation after first auth
